# Private MediaWiki Instance Support

## What
Test and document the engine against self-hosted/private MediaWiki instances (corporate wikis, institutional knowledge bases, private fan wikis). Requires: authentication support (OAuth, basic auth, API tokens) via config, documentation for connecting to non-public instances, and integration tests against a local MediaWiki container.

## Why
Private wikis are the natural enterprise extension path — if Varia handles a corporate wiki behind auth, it proves the adapter boundary works for paid NextConsensus deployments. The `--api` flag already accepts any MediaWiki endpoint, but has no authentication support.

## Context
Read first:
- `packages/ingestion/src/mediawiki-client.ts` — MediaWikiClient constructor, API call pattern
- `packages/cli/src/index.ts` — CLI dispatch, `--api` flag
- `packages/persistence/src/index.ts` — PersistenceAdapter for local storage

## Implementation
1. Add `MediaWikiClient` auth support:
   - `--api-key <token>` flag for bearer token auth
   - `--api-user <user> --api-password <pass>` for basic auth
   - OAuth2 support via `--oauth-client` and `--oauth-secret` env vars
2. Verify against a local MediaWiki container:
   - Docker-based test setup (docker-compose with MediaWiki)
   - Seed with sample pages
   - Run analyze pipeline through auth
3. Document private instance setup in README or new docs file
4. Add integration tests

## Invariants
- Auth flags are optional — public wikis work without any changes
- Credentials never logged or exposed in error messages
- Docker tests are opt-in (require Docker, skipped by default)

## Acceptance
- [ ] Token-based auth: `--api-key` flag works against protected wiki
- [ ] Basic auth: `--api-user` + `--api-password` works
- [ ] Docker compose test: local MediaWiki container with auth
- [ ] All existing public wiki tests still pass
- [ ] Documentation covers private instance setup
- [ ] Gate: build, lint, typecheck, test
